'''Andy Johnson''' (born 14 June 1974) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played at club level for the [[Wigan Warriors]], [[Huddersfield Giants]], [[London Broncos]], [[Castleford Tigers]] and the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]]. A versatile [[utility player]], Johnson played in a number of positions throughout his career, usually as a [[centre (rugby league)|centre]] or a [[back row (rugby league)|back row]] forward.<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref name="Castleford RLFC A to Z Player List (All Time)">{{cite web|url=http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/alltime.php|title=Castleford RLFC A to Z Player List (All Time)|publisher=thecastlefordtigers.co.uk c/o web.archive.org|date=31 December 2013|accessdate=1 January 2014|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120216084010/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/alltime.php|archivedate=16 February 2012}}</ref><ref name="Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk">{{cite web|url=http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=77|title=Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk|publisher=thecastlefordtigers.co.uk c/o web.archive.org|date=31 December 2013|accessdate=1 January 2014|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120722034259/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=77|archivedate=22 July 2012}}</ref>

==Background==
Andy Johnson was born in [[Wigan]], [[Greater Manchester]], England, and as of 2013 he lives with his wife Alex and two sons Jack and James and is raising money to fight [[Duchenne muscular dystrophy]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://joiningjack.org/menu/about-aj.aspx |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120825230256/http://joiningjack.org/menu/about-aj.aspx |url-status=dead |archive-date=25 August 2012 |title=About A.J. |publisher=Joining Jack |accessdate=16 August 2013 }}</ref>

==Playing career==
Andy Johnson joined [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] aged 17, he won 100 first games by playing in both the [[Rugby league positions#Backs|backs]] and the [[Rugby league positions#Forwards|forwards]]. In 1997 he participated in two [[Rugby League Premiership|Premiership]] Finals at [[Old Trafford]], [[Manchester]]. The same year he played for [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[World Club Challenge]] against the [[Canterbury Bulldogs]] which ended up with him being the 9th highest scorer. In December 1997, Johnson ruptured an [[Achilles tendon rupture|achilles tendon]] in training, keeping him out for the entirety of 1998's [[Super League III]].{{citation needed|date=August 2021}} In August 1999, Johnson was loaned out to the [[Huddersfield Giants]] for the rest of the season.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Hadfield |first1=Dave |title=Rugby League: Eagles set to leave Sheffield |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-eagles-set-to-leave-sheffield-1112662.html |archive-url=https://ghostarchive.org/archive/20220507/https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-eagles-set-to-leave-sheffield-1112662.html |archive-date=7 May 2022 |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |website=The Independent |accessdate=29 June 2019 |date=14 August 1999}}{{cbignore}}</ref>

At the end of the 1999's [[Super League IV]], Johnson and Wigan Warriors' teammate [[Jon Clarke (rugby league)|Jon Clarke]] both signed a two-year deal with [[London Broncos]], re-uniting with former Wigan coach [[John Monie]].<ref>{{cite web |last1=Hadfield |first1=Dave |title=Rugby League: Monie recruits young Wigan pair for Broncos |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-monie-recruits-young-wigan-pair-for-broncos-1129855.html |archive-url=https://ghostarchive.org/archive/20220507/https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-monie-recruits-young-wigan-pair-for-broncos-1129855.html |archive-date=7 May 2022 |url-access=subscription |url-status=live |website=The Independent |accessdate=29 June 2019 |date=30 November 1999}}{{cbignore}}</ref> He signed a contract with [[Castleford Tigers]] in August 2001.<ref>{{cite web |title=Castleford sign Johnson |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/1513918.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=29 June 2019 |date=28 August 2001}}</ref> He quit playing after suffering an injury which he got 14-months after he signed a two-year contract with the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]].{{citation needed|date=February 2020}}
